title: Dictionary Methods
content:
### Dictionary Methods Reference

---

### Access Methods

| Method | Description | Example |
|--------|-------------|---------|
| `.get(key, default)` | Safe access with default | `d.get("x", 0)` |
| `.keys()` | View of all keys | `d.keys()` |
| `.values()` | View of all values | `d.values()` |
| `.items()` | View of all (key, value) pairs | `d.items()` |
| `.setdefault(key, default)` | Set if key missing, return value | `d.setdefault("x", 0)` |

---

### Modify Methods

| Method | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `.update(dict2)` | Merge another dict |
| `.pop(key, default)` | Remove key, return value |
| `.popitem()` | Remove and return last inserted |
| `.clear()` | Remove all items |

---

### Copy Method

| Method | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `.copy()` | Shallow copy of dictionary |

---

### `fromkeys()` -- Create from Keys

```python
keys = ["a", "b", "c"]
new_dict = dict.fromkeys(keys, 0)
print(new_dict)  # {"a": 0, "b": 0, "c": 0}
```

---

### Built-in Functions

```python
d = {"a": 1, "b": 2, "c": 3}

print(len(d))       # 3
print(sorted(d))    # ["a", "b", "c"] (sorts keys)
print(any(d))       # True (checks if any key is truthy)
print(all(d))       # True (checks if all keys are truthy)
```

---

### Operator Support

```python
d1 = {"a": 1, "b": 2}
d2 = {"b": 3, "c": 4}

print(d1 | d2)     # Merge (Python 3.9+)
print(len(d1))     # 2
print("a" in d1)   # True
print("z" not in d1)  # True
```

> **Key Point:** `.keys()`, `.values()`, and `.items()` return **view objects** that reflect changes to the dictionary. Use `list()` to capture a static snapshot.
codeExample:
student = {"name": "Alice", "age": 25, "grade": "A"}

print("Keys:", list(student.keys()))
print("Values:", list(student.values()))
print("Items:", list(student.items()))

# fromkeys
keys = ["x", "y", "z"]
result = dict.fromkeys(keys, 0)
print("fromkeys:", result)

# setdefault
student.setdefault("city", "Boston")
print("setdefault:", student)
